items.problem: RecordReview deduplicates findings by id within a run using first-occurrence-wins, but the pool findings.txt is a concatenation of per-reviewer rows with no guaranteed ordering; the "first" record's severity/category may not be the consensus severity
ParseSince parses "2w" as 14 days using float64 multiplication (n * float64(per)), which can produce sub-nanosecond drift for large week counts due to IEEE 754 rounding
Append assumes O_APPEND atomicity for multi-KB batches

Append serializes all records to memory then writes with a single O_APPEND call, claiming POSIX atomicity; POSIX guarantees atomicity only for writes up to PIPE_BUF (typically 4096 bytes) on pipes/FIFOs, not regular files
TestRecordReview_AppendsOneRecordPerPoolFinding asserts record count but does not verify that the second record's fields (Package, Severity, File, Category, ID) match the input

Load skips malformed JSON lines silently with `continue`, so a ledger corrupted by a partial write silently drops records with no diagnostic; the caller (cmd/atcr/history.go) has no way to know data was lost
normalizeSeverity is a duplicate of reconcile.NormalizeSeverity

RenderTable normalizes empty severity to "UNKNOWN" but the canonical column set is fixed to CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW; an "UNKNOWN" severity column is appended as an extra, but the normalizeSeverity call on line 46 is unreachable if the caller always passes a non-empty severity
